name: Flourish
short_description: Find your rhythm in the cycle of the garden: prune to gather, water to renew, and trade your clippings for deep-rooted progression.
detailed_description:
Cut mature plants to gather clippings, water the soil to spark new growth, and spend your harvest on an upgrade tree that deepens every part of the cycle. Unlock wider tool reach, faster regrowth, and new species to cultivate as your garden fills out. Clovers give way to wildflowers, bushes, and eventually trees.

Summon waterflies to tend your garden alongside you, watering the soil while you focus on the harvest.

Once your garden is fully grown, prestige to start fresh with a wider plot and lasting bonuses. Each cycle, your garden reaches a little further.

Flourish is a relaxed, unhurried incremental. There's always something ready to harvest, always something worth growing toward.
about_the_game:

reviews.review_text:
it's a clicker game that takes 20 mins to finish...
and literally no content, you just cut grass...
no prestige system, no meaningful goals or upgrades...
and on top of that this game is not free... 

is it early access or something?

The soil is dry, the air is still,
To satisfy a digital will.
One thousand seeds in rows of gray,
To waste the hours of the day.

The water drips in measured beats,
Through endless rows and repetitive sheets.
A flower blooms, then fades from view,
To buy a spade that’s slightly new.

The colors bleed to duller tones,
Among the quiet, clicking drones.
A garden built of heavy chores,
Behind the screen and locked-tight doors.

No scent of Earth, no breath of spring,
Just the tally that the harvests bring.
A hundred buds for a single cent,
Until the spark of life is spent.

Very short game.
25 min to 100%
there is no automation. no fairy drones, no sprinklers.
just some upgrades.

at least it looks cute.
This game was incredibly short, 29 minutes for me to beat it. I think that's a bit weak bruv. Also the aim of the water was off.
It is a very cozy game with nice graphics but it is just way too short. I 100%ed the game in 19 minutes of gameplay. Game has great potential.
